explanations.10: useBlogPost() is a hook (a special function) that gives us information about whether we're currently viewing a single blog post or a list of posts.
explanations.11: This returns a CSS class name for spacing if we're in list view, or undefined (nothing) if we're viewing a single post—it's a ternary operator, a shorthand if/else statement.
explanations.16: clsx() is a utility function that combines multiple CSS class names together, ignoring any that are undefined or false, so we don't end up with extra spaces in our class attribute.
